metaDescription: Learn the basics of Freshdesk's ticketing system, from receiving requests to resolving and closing tickets.
intro: Freshdesk's ticketing system is the core of how support teams manage customer requests. This guide covers the basic workflow from ticket creation to resolution.

steps.heading: Understand where tickets come from
steps.body: Tickets can arrive via email, the customer portal, chat, or other connected channels, and all land in a central queue.

steps.heading: Triage incoming tickets
steps.body: Review new tickets, set priorities, and assign them to the right agent or group.

steps.heading: Work the ticket
steps.body: Reply to the customer, add internal notes for teammates, and update the status as progress is made.

steps.heading: Resolve and close
steps.body: Mark the ticket resolved once the issue is fixed, and it will close automatically or after customer confirmation depending on your settings.

faq.question: What's the difference between resolved and closed?
faq.answer: Resolved typically means the fix is in place, while closed means the ticket is fully finished and no further action is expected.

faq.question: Can I reopen a closed ticket?
faq.answer: Yes, closed tickets can usually be reopened if the customer responds again about the same issue.

faq.question: How do I prioritize tickets?
faq.answer: Freshdesk lets you set priority levels manually or through automation rules based on criteria you define.

faq.question: Can multiple agents work on the same ticket?
faq.answer: While a ticket is typically assigned to one agent, others can add internal notes or be looped in for visibility.
